items.notes: The most common word for car; also used in compounds like 'autokauppa' (car dealership)
examples: Minulla on punainen auto. — I have a red car.

items.notes: Borrowed from English; commonly used in everyday Finnish
examples: Bussi lähtee kello kahdeksan. — The bus leaves at eight o'clock.

items.notes: VR (Valtion Rautatiet) operates Finland's trains
examples: Juna Helsinkiin lähtee raiteelta kolme. — The train to Helsinki departs from track three.

items.notes: Compound word: 'lento' (flight) + 'kone' (machine), literally 'flight machine'
examples: Lentokone laskeutui turvallisesti. — The airplane landed safely.

items.notes: Compound word: 'polku' (pedal/path) + 'pyörä' (wheel), literally 'pedal wheel'. Often shortened to 'pyörä' in casual speech.
examples: Hän ajaa polkupyörällä töihin. — He/she rides a bicycle to work.

items.notes: Used for large vessels; ferries between Finland and Sweden/Estonia are very popular. A smaller boat is 'vene'.
examples: Laiva seilasi Helsingistä Tukholmaan. — The ship sailed from Helsinki to Stockholm.

items.notes: Compound word: 'raitio' (rail/track) + 'vaunu' (wagon/car). Often shortened to 'ratikka' in spoken Helsinki Finnish.
examples: Raitiovaunu pysähtyy tässä. — The tram stops here.

items.notes: Helsinki has the northernmost metro system in the world
examples: Metro kulkee Itäkeskukseen. — The metro goes to Itäkeskus.

items.notes: Same word in many languages; taxis in Finland are typically pre-booked or found at stands
examples: Soitin taksin. — I called a taxi.

items.notes: General word for road or way; also used figuratively meaning 'path' or 'way'
examples: Tämä tie vie Turkuun. — This road leads to Turku.

items.notes: Refers to a street in a city or town, as opposed to 'tie' which is a road
examples: Asun Mannerheimintiellä — kadulla on paljon kauppoja. — I live on Mannerheimintie — the street has many shops.

items.notes: Finland has thousands of bridges due to its many lakes and rivers. Also used in compound place names.
examples: Silta ylittää joen. — The bridge crosses the river.

items.notes: Used for train stations, bus stations, etc. Helsinki's main station is 'Helsingin päärautatieasema'.
examples: Nähdään asemalla! — See you at the station!

items.notes: Compound word: 'lento' (flight) + 'kenttä' (field), literally 'flight field'. Helsinki-Vantaa is Finland's main airport.
examples: Lentokenttä on kaupungin ulkopuolella. — The airport is outside the city.

items.notes: Important in Finland due to extensive coastline and ferry traffic. Common in place names like 'Eteläsatama' (South Harbor).
examples: Laiva saapui satamaan. — The ship arrived at the harbor.

items.notes: From the verb 'pysähtyä' (to stop). Used for bus stops and tram stops. 'Bussipysäkki' specifies a bus stop.
examples: Pysäkki on tuolla kadun kulmassa. — The bus stop is there at the street corner.

items.notes: Also means 'flag'; context makes it clear. 'Matkalippu' specifically means travel ticket.
examples: Ostin lipun junaan. — I bought a ticket for the train.

items.notes: Can refer to paper maps or digital maps. 'Maailmankartta' means world map.
examples: Katson karttaa. — I'm looking at a/the map.

items.notes: Used for both cities and larger towns in Finnish
examples: Helsinki on Suomen suurin kaupunki. — Helsinki is Finland's largest city.

items.notes: A small rural community. Finnish countryside has many small 'kylät' (villages, plural).
examples: Hän asuu pienessä kylässä. — He/she lives in a small village.

items.notes: From 'keski' (middle/center). 'Kaupungin keskusta' means city center.
examples: Mennään keskustaan! — Let's go to the center!

items.notes: Very common word; also means 'trade' or 'deal'. 'Ruokakauppa' = grocery store.
examples: Kauppa sulkeutuu kello yhdeksältä. — The shop closes at nine o'clock.

items.notes: Finnish schools are renowned worldwide for their education system
examples: Lapset menevät kouluun. — The children go to school.

items.notes: Most Finns belong to the Evangelical Lutheran Church. Famous churches include Temppeliaukion kirkko (Rock Church) in Helsinki.
examples: Kirkko on kaupungin keskustassa. — The church is in the city center.

items.notes: From 'sairas' (sick/ill) + '-la' (place suffix). Healthcare in Finland is publicly funded.
examples: Hän on sairaalassa. — He/she is in the hospital.

items.notes: Borrowed from Swedish/German. Major Finnish banks include Nordea and OP.
examples: Pankki avautuu kello yhdeksältä. — The bank opens at nine o'clock.

items.notes: Refers to both the postal service and mail itself. Posti is also the name of Finland's national postal company.
examples: Vien paketin postiin. — I'll take the package to the post office.

items.notes: From 'kirja' (book) + '-sto' (collection suffix). Finland has one of the highest library usage rates in the world. Helsinki's Oodi library is world-famous.
examples: Kirjasto on auki joka päivä. — The library is open every day.

items.notes: From 'ravinto' (nourishment/nutrition) + '-la' (place suffix)
examples: Syödään ravintolassa tänään. — Let's eat at a restaurant today.

items.notes: Borrowed from French/English. Common alternatives in Finland include 'majatalo' (guesthouse) and 'mökki' (cabin).
examples: Hotelli on lähellä keskustaa. — The hotel is near the center.

items.notes: Helsinki has many museums including Ateneum (art), Kansallismuseo (national museum), and Kiasma (contemporary art).
examples: Museo on suljettu maanantaisin. — The museum is closed on Mondays.

items.notes: Finnish cities have many green spaces; 'kansallispuisto' means national park
examples: Lapset leikkivät puistossa. — The children play in the park.
